rationale: This page contains detailed information on several Indian defense technologies including the Bhargavastra anti-drone system, the Gaurav long-range glide bomb, fiber optic drones, and Project 17A stealth frigates. These are distinct, high-value topics for generating technical and current affairs questions.
cleanedText:
VTOL UAVs can take off and land vertically, eliminating the need for runways, and providing manoeuvrability and versatility in operations.
Features:
- Equipped with precision-guided anti-personnel warheads.
- Can relay back live video and return to the launch position in automated mode.
6.9.2. BHARGAVASTRA
India has successfully test-fired an indigenously developed anti-drone system, Bhargavastra.
About Bhargavastra
- A low-cost, 'hard kill' anti-drone system designed to neutralize threats from drone swarms.
- Two-layer security system:
- Layer 1: Unguided micro-rockets with a 20m lethal radius for swarm drones
- Layer 2: Guided micro-missiles for precision strikes
6.9.3. LONG-RANGE GLIDE BOMB 'GAURAV'
DRDO successfully conducts release trials of indigenously developed Long-Range Glide Bomb 'Gaurav'.
- Glide bomb after launch steer towards the target using highly accurate hybrid navigation scheme with a combination of Indian Navigation System (INS) and Global Positioning System (GPS) data.
About Gaurav
- Weight: 1,000 kg class
- Range: ‘Gaurav’ achieved 100 km range with pinpoint accuracy.
6.9.4. FIBER OPTIC DRONES
Recently used in the Russia-Ukraine conflict, fiber optic drones are seen as a game-changer in drone warfare due to their resistance to electronic warfare (EW).
About Fiber Optic Drones
- Operated using long fiber optic cables instead of radio links.
- Fiber optic cable is made up of a collection of glass tubes spun as thin as hairs.
- They can support faster speeds, longer distances, and simultaneous communications.
- A single fiber can carry multiple signals simultaneously on different wavelengths
- No radio link means they cannot be jammed or intercepted by EW systems.
6.9.5. PROJECT 17A
Two multi-mission stealth frigates of Project 17A – INS Udaygiri and INS Himgiri, were commissioned into the Indian Navy.
About Project 17 A
- Follow-on ships of Shivalik-class (Project 17) frigates.
- Under it, Seven Frigates being indigenously built by Mazagon Dock (Mumbai) and GRSE (Kolkata).
- Configured with Combined Diesel or Gas (CODOG) main propulsion plants, comprising a Diesel Engine and Gas turbine.
